reasoning: Based on climate data up to July/August 2025, it is extremely unlikely that 2025 will be the hottest year on record. The current record is held by 2024. Expert analyses by Carbon Brief and NASA's Gavin Schmidt from mid-2025 conclude that 2025 is tracking to be the second or third-warmest year on record. Specifically, the NOAA June 2025 Global Climate Report indicated that June 2025 was only the third-warmest June on record, trailing behind 2024 and 2023. With the dissipation of El Niño and the return of ENSO-neutral (and potentially La Niña) conditions, global average temperature anomalies are decreasing relative to the record-shattering peaks of late 2023 and 2024. Consequently, NOAA and other meteorological agencies assess the statistical probability of 2025 claiming the all-time record as "very unlikely." I am assigning a 3% probability, accounting for extreme unforeseen anomalies in the latter half of the year, but deeply anchoring on the established downward temperature trend for the year.
